subject: Bug handoff: REMOTE uninstall ignores the 'delete files from disk' checkbox (you own downloadManager.js)
body:
Handing you this bug — you're live in downloadManager.js with fresher context, and it's the same file. Diagnosed, not fixed (left it to you to avoid colliding).

REPRO (user hit it live, remote mode on an L4): uninstalled 'Wan 2.2 T2V Smooth' with 'delete files from disk' UNCHECKED, expecting the ~12GB UNet weights to stay on the Pod volume. Instead the model dropped to ~6.5GB/36.5GB PARTIALLY INSTALLED — the weights were deleted from the volume anyway. ~30GB lost (already gone; this fix only prevents recurrence).

ROOT CAUSE — routes/downloadManager.js POST /comfy/models/uninstall:
- Line 1159 destructures `deleteFiles = true` (default true).
- The LOCAL branch HONORS it correctly at lines 1294-1298: `if (!deleteFiles && isInModelsFolder) { keptModelFiles.push(...); continue; }` — keeps the file, skips the trash.
- The REMOTE branch (the `if (remoteModels.isRemoteActive())` block, ~lines 1171-1230) NEVER reads `deleteFiles`. It calls `remoteModels.remoteUninstallDep(dep)` for every non-shared, non-universal dep unconditionally. The wrapper's POST /wrapper/models/delete DOES exist (wrapper.py:1248 — the old 'no delete endpoint' note is stale), so it really deletes from the volume. => unchecking the box is silently ignored in remote mode.

SUGGESTED FIX (mirror the local semantics into the remote loop, ~line 1194): inside `for (const dep of dependencies)`, AFTER the universal-skip (1195) and shared-skip (1199) but BEFORE the `remoteUninstallDep` call (1204), add:
  if (!deleteFiles && dep.type !== 'custom_nodes') { keptModelFiles.push({ depId: dep.id, depName: dep.name || dep.id }); continue; }
Then include keptModelFiles in the `download:uninstalled` broadcast (line 1228) + the response (1229) like the local branch does (currently the remote branch hardcodes keptModelFiles:[]). Net: keep=files-stay-on-volume, just drop the install record / let the UI reflect 'not installed' while the bytes remain (so a re-install is near-instant). Decide whether the UI should then show 'partially installed' or a distinct 'on disk, not registered' state — that's a product call.

NOTES:
- This is the same family as the [[project_remote_route_branch_audit]] memory (remote routes touching model files must branch correctly). The branch EXISTS here; it just forgot the deleteFiles flag.
- No image/wrapper change needed — app-side only.
- I did NOT edit the file (you own it). If you'd rather I take it, say so and I'll coordinate a handoff. Consider a new card (MPI-99?) if you want it tracked separately from MPI-97's SSE-reconnect work.
